Title: A squalene synthase-like enzyme initiates production of tetraterpenoid hydrocarbons in Botryococcus braunii Race L

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase-like (SSL) enzyme; contains five conserved SS activity domains, transmembrane domain, NADPH-binding residues
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP); also uses farnesyl diphosphate (FPP) and phytyl diphosphate (PPP) in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Lycopaoctaene; also squalene from FPP and low lycopadiene from PPP in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: First committed step of lycopadiene pathway; tetraterpenoid hydrocarbon branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes head-to-head condensation of two GGPP molecules via a cyclopropyl diphosphate intermediate (PLPP), followed by NADPH-dependent rearrangement/reduction to form lycopaoctaene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate committed precursor of lycopadiene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrocarbon biofuel feedstock; petroleum-equivalent fuel precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Intracellular oil bodies and predominantly extracellular matrix of colonies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates lycopadiene biosynthesis by producing lycopaoctaene from GGPP; major causal enzyme for C40 hydrocarbon production in race L
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOS was identified as a new SS-like enzyme from B. braunii race L that converts GGPP to lycopaoctaene. 2. Cell-free assays showed lycopaoctaene formation from GGPP, but no detectable lycopadiene formation from PPP in algal extracts, supporting the GGPP-to-lycopaoctaene route as the biologically relevant first committed step. 3. Recombinant LOS produced lycopaoctaene from GGPP, squalene from FPP, and low levels of lycopadiene from PPP in vitro, demonstrating unusual substrate promiscuity. 4. Yeast coexpression with AtGGPPS11 enabled in vivo lycopaoctaene production, and LOS complemented yeast erg9 knockout, confirming retained squalene synthase capacity. 5. LOS reaction proceeds through the PLPP cyclopropyl intermediate and is NADPH-preferred and squalestatin-sensitive, analogous to SS chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; recombinant protein enzyme assays; GC-MS; steady-state kinetics; yeast heterologous expression with AtGGPPS11; complementation of yeast erg9 knockout; inhibitor assay with squalestatin; intermediate trapping and phosphatase conversion to PLOH
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first committed and key initiating step of lycopadiene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous use of FPP and PPP can generate alternative C30 and C35 hydrocarbons in vitro; in heterologous systems may divert flux to squalene unless GGPP supply/channeling is ensured
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome of race L from days 0, 3, 7, 14, 21 and 28 over culture cycle; accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Culture growth cycle; enzyme assays dependent on NADPH/NADH and inhibited by squalestatin
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtGGPPS11 (coexpressed in yeast); putative unknown reductase enzyme(s) downstream not identified
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In yeast, GAL10 inducible promoter for LOSΔ391-444 in pESC-TRP; constitutive promoter in XURA for full-length LOS complementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme for metabolic engineering of hydrocarbon biofuel production in heterologous hosts; should be paired with enhanced GGPP supply such as GGPPS and possibly pathway channeling to favor C40 products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LOS is the only tested SS/SSL enzyme able to use all three natural prenyl diphosphate substrates and produce significant tetraterpene, triterpene, and sesquarterpene-related products. In vivo evidence supports GGPP as the relevant substrate despite PPP and FPP promiscuity in vitro.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase (SS); contains conserved SS catalytic domains, transmembrane domain, NADPH-binding residues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH-dependent head-to-head condensation and rearrangement of two FPP molecules to produce squalene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes as related paralog but not the committed enzyme for lycopadiene production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrocarbon biofuel feedstock; petroleum-equivalent fuel precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Intracellular oil bodies and extracellular matrix for lycopadiene, though LSS itself supports sterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a true squalene synthase rather than lycopaoctaene synthase; supports distinction of LOS specialization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LSS was identified as one of two SS-like cDNAs in race L. 2. Recombinant LSS used FPP to produce squalene as its sole product. 3. LSS did not produce lycopaoctaene from GGPP or lycopadiene from PPP under tested conditions. 4. LSS restored ergosterol prototrophy in yeast erg9 knockout, confirming bona fide squalene synthase function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; recombinant protein enzyme assay; GC-MS; yeast erg9 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for target hydrocarbon pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could compete for prenyl precursor flux toward sterol synthesis rather than specialized hydrocarbon production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome of race L; accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In yeast complementation, constitutive promoter in XURA for full-length LSS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as comparator/control SS; may be relevant when balancing sterol needs versus engineered hydrocarbon production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally characterized gene because it was identified together with LOS and directly tested to distinguish true SS from specialized SSL activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Supplies GGPP precursor pool for downstream GGPP-derived isoprenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrocarbon biofuel feedstock; petroleum-equivalent fuel precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced GGPP supply in yeast enabled detectable in vivo lycopaoctaene production when coexpressed with LOS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGGPPS11 was coexpressed with LOS in yeast because native yeast GGPP supply is limiting. 2. Coexpression with LOS yielded lycopaoctaene in vivo, whereas AtGGPPS11 alone did not. 3. The study used AtGGPPS11 as a precursor-supply gene to validate LOS function in heterologous yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast coexpression and GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Acts as precursor-supply bottleneck relief in heterologous yeast system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May increase flux to multiple GGPP-derived products in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast expression system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 inducible promoter for AtGGPPS11Δ57 in pESC-TRP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking partner with LOS to boost tetraterpenoid hydrocarbon production in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as heterologous precursor-support gene rather than endogenous race L biosynthetic gene, but was directly used in functional validation of hydrocarbon production.
